evals.prompt: A new prospect just booked a discovery call. They're a B2B SaaS company at $8M ARR. Their CRM is Salesforce with HubSpot for marketing. They've mentioned they struggle to show marketing's impact on pipeline. How should I frame the discovery conversation using RevOps Global's methodology?
evals.expected_output: Response frames the conversation around the Four Foundations/Pillars. Identifies Source/Campaign Attribution as the primary pillar to address. References RevOps Global's specific approach: Opportunity Influence over single-touch, two-dashboard model, UTM strategy. Uses correct product/team naming. Mentions relevant KB topics to explore.

assertions.name: references-four-foundations
assertions.description: Response explicitly uses the Four Foundations or Four Pillars framework to frame the approach

assertions.name: identifies-attribution-as-primary
assertions.description: Attribution Modeling (Pillar 2/3 per the framework) is called out as the relevant pillar given 'marketing impact on pipeline'

assertions.name: uses-correct-methodology
assertions.description: Mentions multi-touch attribution, Opportunity Influence, or two-dashboard approach - RevOps Global's specific methodology, not generic advice

assertions.name: correct-naming-conventions
assertions.description: Uses RevOps Global OS / RevOps Global AI naming, not RGOS or revops-init

evals.prompt: What is RevOps Global's team structure and which team members own what? And what's the difference between RevOps Global OS and RevOps Global AI?
evals.expected_output: Team table with Greg, Kristina, Rachit, Anumeet and their roles. Clear distinction between RevOps Global OS (hub.revopsglobal.com - internal ops platform) and RevOps Global AI (revopsglobal.ai - client-facing analytics). Notes that RGOS and revops-init are internal shorthands only.

assertions.name: correct-team-members
assertions.description: Lists Greg Harned (CEO), Kristina (HubSpot Strategist), Rachit (HubSpot Architect), Anumeet (Salesforce Architect)

assertions.name: distinguishes-two-products
assertions.description: Clearly explains RevOps Global OS vs RevOps Global AI as separate products at different domains

assertions.name: flags-internal-shorthands
assertions.description: Notes that RGOS and revops-init are internal only and not for client-facing use
